target: PHIL-23: Swarm is a multi-layer filter cascade — every operation is filtering, performance = filtering performance
drop_criterion: DROP if layer failures demonstrated to NOT propagate downstream (n>=5 incidents)
expect: PHIL-23 claims all filter failures propagate downstream. Expect >=5 incidents where failures were contained -> partial falsification.
actual: PARTIALLY FALSIFIED. Found 8 distinct incident classes (n>=12 total events) where filter layer failures were contained and did NOT propagate downstream. CASCADE is real but CONDITIONAL on absence of structural enforcement gates.
diff: Prediction 'all failures propagate' is wrong. Correct model: failures propagate IFF no structural gate exists at detection point. Containment requires: (1) structural enforcement at detection point, (2) diagnosis+repair in same session, (3) multi-tool coverage.

contained_failures.description: A->K cascade caught at pre-commit gate, did NOT propagate to downstream sessions
FM-19 false blocks (session identity misidentification at N>=3) prevented from corrupting downstream work
claim.py prevented 6-file collision from propagating to concurrent sessions
Three consecutive false instruments detected and fixed before contaminating >50 downstream lessons
209-session-stale baseline detected and fixed before triggering 240+ sessions of cascading false outputs
Cascade monitor reduces detection latency 35x (56 sessions -> 1.6 seconds), catching cascades before propagation
FM-19 collisions actively blocked at pre-commit 50% of the time, preventing merge
Domain regex bug in 10 independent parsers caught in single audit session before producing >1 session of false outputs
contained_failures.containment_mechanism: Audit + centralized parsing (lesson_meta.py) + format-migration test in check.sh

propagated_failures.description: T->Q->A cascade was silent for 240 sessions before detection
propagated_failures.note: Pre-cascade_monitor era — demonstrates that WITHOUT structural gates, PHIL-23 cascade prediction holds

containment_conditions: Structural enforcement gate wired at detection point (check.sh, orient.py, claim.py)
Detection tool output feeds into blocking action, not just measurement
Diagnosis + repair happen in same session (L-1211)
Multi-tool coverage exists for different failure modes (L-653)

verdict: PHIL-23 PARTIALLY FALSIFIED: CASCADE IS CONDITIONAL. The claim that 'a failure at one layer propagates to corrupt downstream layers' is true only when no structural enforcement gate exists between layers. When gates exist, n>=8 incident classes demonstrate containment. Revised claim: 'Filter failures propagate downstream UNLESS structural gates exist at layer boundaries. Ungated layers cascade; gated layers contain.' DROP criterion MET (n=8 > n>=5 threshold).
recommended_action: REFINE PHIL-23: add conditionality. File first-ever challenge against PHIL-23.
